comments_critique.summary: The discussion centers around a new browser-based grand strategy game supporting hundreds of players on large maps. Comments include feedback on the game's art style, user experience with the queue system, and technical insights into the game's backend performance and architecture.

comments.author: sgolem
comments.text:
One of the main challenges has been making the map simulation work for hundreds of simultaneous players without the server becoming a bottleneck.

Most interactions are event-driven and the map state updates incrementally, which keeps things manageable even when many borders are shifting at once.

I've successfully tested this with 4096^2 maps and 1024 players and in those tests framerates were stable at 144 FPS, with the server ticking steadily at the standard 10 TPS.

It is built on Rust and Bevy. I'm happy to answer any questions.
comments.analysis: This comment provides detailed technical information about the game's backend and performance, indicating a deep understanding and likely direct involvement with the project.

comments.author: brudgers
comments.text:
I clicked "Play Game" and got a message about a queue.

Maybe that experience can be improved.
comments.analysis: This comment offers user experience feedback that is plausible and relevant, suggesting a potential area for improvement in the game's onboarding process.

comments.author: beeflet
comments.text: I don't like the mixel art used
comments.analysis: This comment expresses a subjective opinion about the art style without providing constructive feedback or technical insight.
